summary: Addresses gndrmnn's external review #4 feedback (CC-0087) by removing a tautological assertion that compared the self-declared `KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey` constant to its literal string value `"spec.secretRefs.name"` in `TestKeystoneSecretIndexerKey_IsDeclaredAsConst`. The test's docstring was narrowed to describe the remaining behavior-focused scope: verifying that `registerSecretNameIndex` sources its index key from the exported constant and registers it against the Keystone type. A review-pattern file was added at `.planwerk/review_patterns/avoid-testing-literal-values-of-self-declared-constants.md` to capture the learning. The progress state was advanced from `awaiting_external_review` to `improving`, and the external review artifact was persisted at `.planwerk/reviews/CC-0087-github-review-by-gndrmnn-external-4.json`.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (pre-review Lint/Vet report 0 issues; gosec and stub_scanner pass)
Tautological literal-value assertion removed per reviewer feedback
Remaining assertions are behavior-focused (error-free registration, single IndexField call, key sourced from exported const, registered against Keystone type)
Docstring accurately describes the narrowed test scope
Sibling test TestRegisterSecretNameIndex_WrapsErrorWithKey remains intact (no collateral damage)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (Gomega assertions via NewGomegaWithT, matches gomega-test-assertions-with-newgomegawitht pattern)
No dead code or commented-out leftovers
Docstring includes CC-0087 feature ID and REQ-006 reference per FEATURE_ID_REQUIRED rule
Minimal, scope-aligned change (only lines 3279-3294 of keystone_controller_test.go touched plus planwerk artifacts)

security_checklist.item: gosec scan on changed files reports no issues

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — minimal deletion plus docstring adjustment
No architectural impact; production behavior unchanged
Review pattern captured in .planwerk/review_patterns/ for future detection

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Removed duplicated constant-value restatement (the exact DRY violation flagged by the reviewer)
No speculative additions; only the requested deletion was performed

fail_fast_checklist.item: Test still fails fast if registerSecretNameIndex uses a different key or object type
Test still fails fast if more than one indexer is registered (HaveLen(1))

defensive_checklist.item: Test still defends the invariant that matters (registration key sourced from the exported const)
Removal does not weaken meaningful coverage — the literal-value check was tautological and provided no protection against regressions

next_steps: Merge the change; reviewer feedback is fully resolved.
Update the progress phase accordingly once the cycle completes.
